text:
First, note that time zones as we know them did not exist in 1340.  
But for the purpose of this exercise, we assume modern time zone offsets applied historically.

Athens: EET (UTC+2) or EEST (UTC+3) in modern times.  
October 3 is not in DST in modern rules (DST ends late March to late October? Actually EU DST ends last Sunday Oct, so Oct 3 is still DST in modern EU).  
But historically irrelevant; we'll use modern offsets for calculation.

Assume Athens is UTC+3 (EEST) on Oct 3.  
Tokyo is UTC+9 year-round.

Time difference: UTC+9 − UTC+3 = +6 hours.

4:00 PM Athens = 16:00 Athens time.  
Add 6 hours → 22:00 same date in Tokyo.

Date remains October 3, 1340 in Tokyo (no date crossing because +6h doesn’t pass midnight).

FINAL_ANSWER: 1340-10-03 22:00
